title: Nonlinear Independent Component Analysis
speaker: Aapo Hyvärinen
abstract: Unsupervised learning, in particular learning general nonlinear representations, is one of the deepest problems in machine learning. Estimating latent quantities in a generative model provides a principled framework, and has been successfully used in the linear case, e.g. with independent component analysis (ICA) and sparse coding. However, extending ICA to the nonlinear case has proven to be extremely difficult: A straight-forward extension is unidentifiable, i.e. it is not possible to recover those latent components that actually generated the data.

proof_stages.label: Unsupervised-learning motivation and linear ICA baseline
proof_stages.summary: The talk motivates unsupervised disentanglement from the limits of label-based deep learning and reviews linear ICA as an identifiable latent-variable model, contrasting it with PCA and Gaussian factor analysis.

proof_stages.label: IID nonlinear ICA non-identifiability obstruction
proof_stages.summary: A direct nonlinear extension of ICA with only independent latent components is shown to be ill-defined, since conditional-CDF constructions produce alternative independent coordinates with the same observable distribution.

proof_stages.label: Nonstationary sources and time-contrastive learning
proof_stages.summary: Replacing IID sampling by nonstationary time-series structure yields extra constraints, and a segment-classification self-supervised objective recovers nonlinear ICA components up to simple residual ambiguities under the stated theorem.

proof_stages.label: Stationary temporal dependence and permutation contrastive learning
proof_stages.summary: For stationary time series, discriminating true adjacent windows from time-permuted windows gives another contrastive objective that is identifiable when latent components have suitable temporal dependencies.

proof_stages.label: Time-series generalizations and practical refinements
proof_stages.summary: The temporal approaches are extended by combining nonstationarity with autoregressive dependence, learning segmentations with hidden Markov models, and making the contrastive objectives more robust to outliers.

proof_stages.label: Auxiliary-variable conditional latent-variable model and IVAE
proof_stages.summary: The temporal mechanisms are unified by an auxiliary variable whose conditioning modulates otherwise conditionally independent latent components, leading to identifiable deep latent-variable models and the IVAE estimator.

proof_stages.label: Alternative estimators and relaxed component assumptions
proof_stages.summary: Beyond IVAE, the speaker discusses noise-free likelihood and relative-gradient estimation, connections with flow and energy-based models, and recent extensions allowing certain dependent components.

proof_stages.label: Principled disentanglement synthesis and methodological positioning
proof_stages.summary: The conclusion and Q&A position identifiable nonlinear ICA as a rigorous probabilistic framework for disentanglement, clarify the roles of PCA-style dimension reduction and self-supervised heuristics, and compare the approach with other factorizations.
